---
title: "Welcome"
subtitle: "Data Science Flipped Classroom - Module 1"
author: "Adriana Clavijo Daza"
date: "2026-03-17"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: slides.scss
    transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
footer: "DSFC - Module 1"
editor: source
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

# About me {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Adriana Clavijo Daza

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Data Scientist & Statistician
- Over 5 years of professional experience
- More than 10 years using R
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
- Data enthusiast
- Open source advocate
:::

::::

# Course introduction {background-color="#4C326A"}

## What is this course about?

A hands-on, project-based program to learn [data analysis and reproducible research]{.highlight-peach} using R and the tidyverse.

. . .

You will learn to:

::: {.incremental}
- Explore, transform, and visualize data
- Write reproducible reports with Quarto
- Use version control with Git and GitHub
- Apply open science and FAIR data principles
- Build a data analysis portfolio
:::

## Course contents

| Module | Topic |
|--------|-------|
| 1 | Welcome & course setup |
| 2 | Data visualization with ggplot2 |
| 3 | Data transformation with dplyr |
| 4 | Data import & spreadsheet organization |
| 5 | Conditions, dates & tables |
| 6 | Data types, vectors & loops |
| 7 | Pivoting & joining data |
| 8 | Publishing with Quarto & GitHub Pages |

# Structure {background-color="#4C326A"}

## How the course works

- **8 modules**, two weeks each
- Bi-weekly live sessions
- Assignments & quizzes
- Capstone project at the end

. . .

::: {.task}
**Good to know**

- Recordings and slides are available on the course website (ignore the original dates)
- Sessions are pre-recorded, so we will use the live sessions for Q&A and discussion
:::

## Calendar {.smaller}

| Module | Start | End | Live session | Time |
|--------|-------|-----|-------------|------|
| Module 1 | Mar 02 | Mar 22 | Mar 17 | 13:00 - 14:00 |
| Module 2 | Mar 23 | Apr 05 | Apr 02 | 14:00 - 16:00 |
| Module 3 | Apr 13 | Apr 26 | Apr 23 | 14:00 - 16:00 |
| Module 4 | Apr 27 | May 10 | May 07 | 14:00 - 16:00 |
| Module 5 | May 11 | May 24 | May 21 | 14:00 - 16:00 |
| Module 6 | May 25 | Jun 07 | Jun 04 | 14:00 - 16:00 |
| Module 7 | Jun 08 | Jun 21 | Jun 18 | 14:00 - 16:00 |
| Module 8 | Jun 22 | Jun 28 | Jun 25 | 14:00 - 16:00 |

## Key dates

| Milestone | Date |
|-----------|------|
| Capstone project due | Jul 13, 2026 |
| Final presentation | Jul 16, 2026 (14:00 - 16:00) |

## Assignments & quizzes {.smaller}

| Module | Assignment due | Quiz due |
|--------|---------------|----------|
| Module 1 | Mar 23 | Mar 23 |
| Module 2 | Apr 13 | Apr 13 |
| Module 3 | Apr 27 | Apr 27 |
| Module 4 | May 11 | May 11 |
| Module 5 | May 25 | May 25 |
| Module 6 | Jun 08 | Jun 08 |
| Module 7 | Jun 22 | -- |
| Module 8 | -- | -- |

# Tools {background-color="#4C326A"}

## Tools we will use

::: {.incremental}
- **Element** -- for communication (we will avoid email)
- **Git** -- for version control
- **GitHub** -- for sharing content and collaboration
- **Posit Cloud** -- for working with R and RStudio
:::

# Git & GitHub {background-color="#4C326A"}

## What is Git?

Git is a [version control system]{.highlight-peach} that tracks changes in your files over time.

. . .

Think of it as an **unlimited undo button** for your projects -- you can go back to any previous version, see what changed, and work on different ideas without losing anything.

## What is GitHub?

GitHub is a [web platform]{.highlight-yellow} that hosts Git repositories online.

. . .

It allows you to:

- Share your code with others
- Collaborate on projects
- Showcase your work as a portfolio
- Access other people's code and data

. . .

::: {.hand-purple}
Git = the tool on your computer. GitHub = the online platform.
:::

# R {background-color="#4C326A"}

## What is R?

R is a [programming language]{.highlight-peach} designed for data analysis, statistics, and visualization.

. . .

Why R?

- **Free and open source**
- Thousands of packages for every type of analysis
- Excellent for creating plots and reports
- Large and supportive community
- Widely used in academia and industry

. . .

We will use **RStudio** as our development environment -- it makes working with R much easier.

# Quarto {background-color="#4C326A"}

## What is Quarto?

Quarto is an [open-source publishing system]{.highlight-yellow} that lets you combine code, text, and results into a single document.

. . .

You can create:

- Reports and articles
- Presentations (like these slides!)
- Websites and blogs
- Books

. . .

::: {.task}
**Why does this matter?**

Quarto enables [reproducible research]{.highlight-peach}: anyone can re-run your analysis and get the same results. No more copy-pasting figures into Word!
:::

## {background-color="#4C326A"}

::: {.hand-purple-large}
Questions?
:::

# Let's get started! {background-color="#4C326A"}
